summary: TermuxAI new connection features fully tested and working: Health check endpoint, Termux auto-setup script endpoint, server.py download endpoint, and auto-detection routing. All 20 backend tests passed (100%), frontend fully functional with auto-routing to terminal when backend is reachable.

issues: Tab elements missing testID attributes (data-testid). Found by text instead. Recommend adding testID='tab-terminal', testID='tab-agent', testID='tab-files', testID='tab-settings' for better testing.

action_items: Optional: Add testID attributes to tab navigation elements for more robust automated testing
critical_code_review_comments: All new endpoints implemented correctly with proper response formats
Health endpoint returns status, storage type (mongodb/json), terminal_active boolean, and version
Termux setup script is comprehensive: includes pkg install, pip install, tmux setup, start/stop/status scripts, auto-restart on crash, wake-lock support
Server.py download endpoint serves the actual server.py file for Termux installation
Auto-detection routing works correctly: checks backend health, then checks config, routes to terminal if config exists, otherwise to onboarding
Connection screen properly handles local/remote modes with polling for backend availability
All existing endpoints still working (config, terminal, chat, files)
Frontend uses proper React Native components and mobile-first design
seed_data_creation: Test files created in /tmp during testing (TEST_new_file.txt, TEST_new_directory, TEST_file_to_delete.txt) - automatically cleaned up by test suite
context_for_next_testing_agent: New connection features tested: GET /api/health (returns status ok, storage type, terminal_active, version), GET /api/termux-setup (returns bash setup script with pkg install, pip install, tmux, start/stop/status scripts), GET /api/download/server.py (returns server.py file content). Frontend auto-detection working: app checks backend health on startup, if reachable checks for config, routes to terminal if config exists (skips connection screen), routes to connection screen if backend unreachable. All 4 tabs working (Terminal, Agent, Files, Settings). Terminal shows live session with history restored from MongoDB.
